name: October 7 Opening Rocket Barrage — ~3,000 Rockets
detail: Hamas launched approximately 3,000 rockets in the opening salvo of the October 7 attack, coordinated with the ground infiltration. Targeted Israeli communities from the Negev to Tel Aviv. Most intercepted by Iron Dome; enough got through to cause mass casualties and suppress Israeli response.

name: Sustained Hamas Rocket Campaign (Oct 2023–Jan 2025)
detail: Hamas maintained rocket fire against southern and central Israel throughout the war despite intensive IDF strikes on launch sites and production. Rockets included Qassam short-range, Grad medium-range, and longer-range missiles reaching Tel Aviv and Haifa.

name: Hamas Kornet & RPG Attacks on IDF Armor
detail: Hamas extensively used Russian-made Kornet anti-tank missiles and tandem-warhead RPGs against IDF Merkava tanks and armored vehicles throughout the ground campaign. Forced IDF to adapt tactics, use bulldozers to clear routes, and rely more on Trophy-equipped armor.

name: Hamas IED Network and Tunnel Ambushes
detail: Hamas deployed thousands of improvised explosive devices along roads and inside structures, causing significant IDF casualties. Fighters used the tunnel network for surprise ambushes on Israeli forces, emerging behind IDF lines to attack and then withdrawing underground.

name: Hamas Drone Attacks on Israeli Forces and Civilians
detail: Hamas deployed explosive drones (quadcopters, fixed-wing) against IDF positions and vehicles, and against Israeli border communities. Drones were used to drop grenades on soldiers and to suppress IDF observation capabilities on October 7. Hamas adapted commercial drone capabilities for military use.

name: Hamas Rocket Attack on Kerem Shalom — 4 IDF Soldiers Killed
detail: On June 1, 2024, Hamas fired rockets at the Kerem Shalom crossing, killing 4 IDF soldiers and wounding 7 others, and damaging aid trucks. Israel temporarily closed the crossing in response. The attack was designed to disrupt humanitarian aid and provoke Israeli reaction.

name: Hezbollah 'Support Front' — 8,000+ Rockets/Drones at Northern Israel
detail: Hezbollah in Lebanon opened a 'support front' for Gaza on October 8, 2023, firing rockets, anti-tank missiles, and drones at northern Israel daily for over a year. Forced evacuation of ~80,000 northern Israelis. Escalated to full conflict in September–November 2024.

name: Iranian Direct Attack — 331 Missiles, Drones & Cruise Missiles
detail: On April 13–14, 2024, Iran fired 170 Shahed drones, 30 cruise missiles, and 120 ballistic missiles directly at Israel in its first-ever direct military strike on Israeli territory. Triggered by an Israeli strike on Iran's Damascus consulate. 99% intercepted by multinational air defenses.

name: Houthi Red Sea & Bab el-Mandeb Attacks
detail: Yemeni Houthi forces attacked over 100 commercial vessels and Israel-linked shipping in the Red Sea starting November 2023. Seized the Galaxy Leader vessel. Disrupted global trade, raising shipping costs 300%+. US-UK conducted retaliatory strikes on Yemen under Operation Prosperity Guardian.

name: Hamas Sniper Operations Against IDF
detail: Hamas deployed trained snipers in urban environments throughout Gaza, targeting IDF soldiers from prepared firing positions. Caused significant IDF casualties in Shejaiya, Khan Younis, and Rafah. Hamas released videos of sniper kills as propaganda throughout the campaign.

name: Palestinian Islamic Jihad (PIJ) Rocket Salvos
detail: PIJ (Islamic Jihad) fired rockets alongside Hamas throughout the war, operating independently but in coordination. PIJ is primarily funded by Iran and maintains its own military wing (Al-Quds Brigades). Fired thousands of rockets from Gaza and occasionally coordinated with Hezbollah.

name: Hamas Tunnel Sabotage Operations
detail: Hamas used its tunnel network not only for movement but for sabotage: detonating explosive devices under IDF forces, collapsing structures with soldiers inside, and ambushing forces that entered tunnel openings. The tunnel environment proved extremely costly for IDF during the early ground operations.

name: Hamas Mortar Fire on IDF Positions
detail: Hamas and PIJ used mortars extensively against IDF staging areas, command positions, and troops in the open. Mortar attacks caused casualties and disrupted operations, particularly in northern Gaza and during the early stages of the ground invasion.

name: Iran Second Ballistic Missile Attack — ~180 Missiles (Oct 1, 2024)
detail: Iran launched approximately 180 ballistic missiles at Israel on October 1, 2024, in retaliation for the killing of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah and other senior figures. Israel and US intercepted the vast majority. One person reported killed. Israel conducted subsequent retaliatory strikes on Iranian military infrastructure.

name: Hamas Hostage Propaganda Videos
detail: Hamas released multiple videos of hostages under duress throughout the war, using them for psychological pressure on Israeli society and international negotiations. Videos of hostages calling on Netanyahu to reach a deal created intense political pressure. Some hostages were shown appearing to condemn Israeli government.
